prompt: A main contractor's QS prepares monthly cash flow forecast for a 24-month £55m project. What is the primary input?

options.text: Programme-linked spend curve, valuation timings, retention, and supply chain payment terms
Only last month's invoice total extrapolated

explanation: Project cash flow forecasting links cost spend to programme and payment cycles.

distractorExplanations.b: Cash flow needs forward programme-based projection.
distractorExplanations.c: Client finances do not drive contractor cost cash flow.
distractorExplanations.d: S-curve spend patterns reflect programme progression.

prompt: Interim valuation under JCT at month 8 should value:

options.text: Full contract sum regardless of progress
Measured work completed, authorised variations, and materials on site per contract rules
Profit for future months not yet worked

explanation: JCT interim valuations reflect executed work and contract valuation rules.

distractorExplanations.a: Interim valuations reflect work done to date.
distractorExplanations.c: Valuations are based on executed work, not future profit.
distractorExplanations.d: Valuation addresses contractor's work, not client liquidity.

prompt: Cost report to employer at month 6 shows forecast final cost £2.1m over budget. Level 3 project finance action?

options.text: Delete contingency to show zero variance
Analyse variance drivers, update forecast, propose mitigations, and seek client decisions on scope/programme

explanation: Level 3 project finance includes proactive forecast control and client decision support.

distractorExplanations.a: Project finance control requires timely transparent reporting.

prompt: S-curve cash flow for construction spend typically shows:

options.text: Slow start, peak mid-project, tapering finish as prelims wind down

explanation: Construction cost cash flow commonly follows an S-curve tied to programme.

distractorExplanations.a: Construction spend follows non-linear programme profile.
distractorExplanations.b: Spend accumulates with site activity progression.
distractorExplanations.c: Peak spend occurs during main construction phase.

prompt: Retention cash flow impact on subcontractor means:

options.text: Reduced monthly cash in until release at PC/defects per subcontract terms
No cash effect because retention is notional

explanation: Retention timing affects supply chain cash flow and must be forecast.

distractorExplanations.b: Retention withholds cash from interim payments.
distractorExplanations.d: Retention is withheld by payer as security.

prompt: Final account preparation on JCT project starts with:

options.text: Agreeing outstanding variations, EOT cost claims, defects, and measured work adjustments
Ignoring all extension of time cost claims
Using tender BOQ only with no variations

explanation: Final accounts reconcile variations, delays, and measured work to contract sum.

distractorExplanations.a: Final account requires full contractual reconciliation.
distractorExplanations.c: Valid LAE claims form part of final account.
distractorExplanations.d: Final account includes all contract adjustments.

prompt: A piled raft foundation is selected over pad foundations on poor ground. Construction technology reason?

options.text: Rafts are always cheaper on rock
Pads preferred when settlement risk is high
Distributes load and reduces differential settlement on variable bearing strata

explanation: Raft foundations address poor ground by distributing structural loads.

distractorExplanations.a: Ground conditions drive foundation selection, not universal cost.
distractorExplanations.b: Rafts address poor/variable ground more effectively.

prompt: CLT (cross-laminated timber) frame advantages in construction technology include:

options.text: Zero need for fire or acoustic design
Offsite precision, faster erection, and lower embodied carbon vs conventional steel/concrete options

explanation: CLT offers programme and carbon benefits with appropriate structural/fire design.

distractorExplanations.a: CLT has structural and fire height constraints per design.
distractorExplanations.b: CLT buildings require fire and acoustic engineering.

prompt: Employer's payment cycle 30 days vs contractor paying subs 45 days creates:

options.text: Working capital gap requiring finance planning in contractor cash flow
Legal requirement for same-day sub payment always

explanation: Payment cycle mismatches drive contractor working capital requirements.

distractorExplanations.b: Payment lag between inflow and outflow affects working capital.
distractorExplanations.c: Payment timing affects cash, not profit directly.
distractorExplanations.d: Payment terms vary by contract subject to statute minima.

prompt: MEP services coordination clash on site — QS cost impact is tracked via:

options.text: Variation or compensation event with measured cost and programme delay assessment
Client absorbs all costs without record

explanation: Site coordination issues are captured through contractual variation mechanisms.

distractorExplanations.a: Coordination failures generate measurable cost/time impacts.
distractorExplanations.d: Cost control requires documented variation/CE valuation.

prompt: Cost-to-complete estimate at 75% progress should include:

options.text: Only original budget with no update
Remaining works quantities, forecast rates, risk, and known claims/defects provision

explanation: Cost-to-complete forecasts update remaining work cost with current intelligence.

distractorExplanations.b: Snagging and defects costs should be considered.
distractorExplanations.d: Remaining work still carries cost to complete.

prompt: Curtain walling system selection — unitised vs stick-built affects:

options.text: Site labour content, programme, tolerances, and logistics cost profile

explanation: Cladding system selection impacts programme, logistics, and installed cost.

distractorExplanations.a: System type affects method, programme, and cost.
distractorExplanations.b: Unitised and stick systems have different cost drivers.
distractorExplanations.c: Cladding system choice is independent of frame material decision.

prompt: RICS QS monthly cost report for employer should present:

options.text: Budget, committed cost, forecast final cost, variances, risks, and cash flow outlook
Contractor payroll confidential data unrelated to project

explanation: Employer cost reports integrate budget, commitment, forecast, and risk.

distractorExplanations.b: Cost reports address financial status and forecast.
distractorExplanations.c: Reports must reflect current forecast and commitments.
distractorExplanations.d: Reports focus on project cost performance.

prompt: Practical completion cash flow effect for contractor often includes:

options.text: Immediate payment of full contract sum if not yet valued
Retention release tranche, reduced prelims, and acceleration of snagging costs

explanation: Practical completion affects retention cash inflow and cost profile.

distractorExplanations.a: Payment follows valuation of completed work.
distractorExplanations.c: PC triggers retention release and cost profile change.
distractorExplanations.d: Payments continue for valued work and releases.

prompt: Ground source heat pump in environmental services selection requires QS to cost:

options.text: Borefield/loop, plant, controls, electrical upgrade, and lifecycle maintenance interfaces

explanation: Environmental services costing includes civil loops, plant, and infrastructure upgrades.

distractorExplanations.a: GSHP systems have distinct civil and MEP cost components.
distractorExplanations.b: Heat pumps often require electrical capacity upgrades.
distractorExplanations.d: Environmental systems interact with building regulations targets.

prompt: CVR (cost value reconciliation) on contractor project shows cost £48m, value £46m at month 10. Meaning?

options.text: Work-in-progress loss — costs exceed certified value; margin erosion requiring action

explanation: Negative CVR gap signals cost over value and margin risk.

distractorExplanations.a: Cost exceeding value indicates margin pressure.
distractorExplanations.b: Value is certified work; lower value means under-recovery.
distractorExplanations.c: CVR is core contractor commercial control.

prompt: Precast concrete flooring vs in-situ slab — construction technology trade-off?

options.text: Precast speeds programme and quality control; in-situ offers flexibility for complex geometry
Precast cannot be used with steel frame

explanation: Flooring system choice balances programme, quality, and design flexibility.

distractorExplanations.b: Selection depends on geometry, programme, and logistics.
distractorExplanations.d: Precast flooring commonly integrates with steel frames.

prompt: Section 278 highway works bond affects project cash flow because:

options.text: Bond/security ties up facility until works certified by highway authority

explanation: Third-party bonds affect working capital and financial planning.

distractorExplanations.a: Bonds are security obligations, not income.
distractorExplanations.c: Bonds affect working capital and contingency.
distractorExplanations.d: Bonds secure specific obligations, not all insurable risks.

prompt: Employer change instruction after 80% MEP installation requires QS to:

options.text: Value variation for dismantle, rework, materials, delay, and testing per contract
Apply 1% of contract sum flat fee

explanation: Late design changes carry dismantle, rework, and delay valuation components.

distractorExplanations.a: Variations include demolition, rework, and delay costs.
distractorExplanations.b: Timely variation valuation supports cost control.
distractorExplanations.d: Valuation must reflect actual change cost.

prompt: Life cycle cost of roof systems should compare:

options.text: Capital, maintenance, replacement, insulation performance, and warranty terms

explanation: Roof option appraisal uses whole-life cost not capital cost alone.

distractorExplanations.a: Life cycle costing includes operational and replacement costs.
distractorExplanations.b: Performance and maintenance drive life cycle cost.
distractorExplanations.c: Roof LCC focuses on roof system costs over life.

prompt: Project bank facility covenant requires quarterly cost report certification. QS role?

options.text: Provide accurate certified cost/forecast data per funder template and covenant definitions

explanation: Funded projects require QS-certified cost reporting per covenant.

distractorExplanations.b: Misreporting breaches ethics and covenant law.
distractorExplanations.d: QS certifies cost data, not structural engineering.

prompt: Tower crane foundation and climbing costs belong in:

options.text: Preliminaries/temporary works with programme-linked duration costing

explanation: Major plant items are costed in preliminaries with programme duration.

distractorExplanations.a: Crane bases are temporary works/prelims unless permanent.
distractorExplanations.d: Crane costs are construction phase preliminaries.

prompt: NEC project bank account (if used) affects cash flow by:

options.text: Ring-fencing payments to contractor and supply chain per trust provisions
Removing need for defined cost records

explanation: NEC PBA mechanisms protect supply chain cash flow from project income.

distractorExplanations.a: PBA manages payment flow, not eliminate it.
distractorExplanations.b: PBA protects supply chain payment from project receipts.
distractorExplanations.d: Defined cost/open book may still apply.

prompt: Damp-proof course and cavity tray detailing in masonry construction prevents:

options.text: Moisture penetration at junctions — defects here drive snagging and warranty claims

explanation: Correct moisture detailing prevents defects with cost/warranty implications.

distractorExplanations.a: DPC/cavity trays address moisture, not primary structure.
distractorExplanations.b: Thermal performance is separate though related building physics.
distractorExplanations.c: Planning is regulatory; DPC is construction detailing.

prompt: Forecast final cost (FFC) at month 12 equals actual to date plus:

options.text: Cost-to-complete for remaining works including risk and known claims

explanation: FFC = cost incurred to date + realistic cost-to-complete.

distractorExplanations.c: FFC is realistic forecast, not original budget.
distractorExplanations.d: Remaining work must be costed to complete.
